subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation — Toll Plaza
stimulus:
From 5 A.M. to 10 A.M., the rate at which vehicles arrive at a certain toll plaza is modeled by
$$A(t) = 82.5te^{-0.5t}$$
vehicles per hour, where $t$ is the number of hours after 5 A.M. The table below gives values of $A(t)$ at selected values of $t$, for $0 \leq t \leq 5$.

| $t$ (hours after 5 A.M.) | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $A(t)$ (vehicles per hour) | 0 | 49.9 | 60.7 | 55.5 | 45.0 | 34.7 |

At time $t = 0$ (5 A.M.), there are no vehicles in line at the toll plaza. The toll plaza processes vehicles at a constant rate of 400 vehicles per hour.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the total number of vehicles that arrive at the toll plaza from 6 A.M. ($t = 1$) to 10 A.M. ($t = 5$).

scoring_points.description: Correct integral expression with limits from $t = 1$ to $t = 5$: $\displaystyle\int_{1}^{5} A(t)\,dt$ or equivalently $\displaystyle\int_{1}^{5} 82.5te^{-0.5t}\,dt$.

parts.prompt: Find the average value of the rate, in vehicles per hour, at which vehicles arrive at the toll plaza from 6 A.M. ($t = 1$) to 10 A.M. ($t = 5$).

scoring_points.description: Sets up the correct average value expression: $\dfrac{1}{5-1}\displaystyle\int_{1}^{5} A(t)\,dt = \dfrac{1}{4}\int_{1}^{5} 82.5te^{-0.5t}\,dt$.
Correct answer: approximately $52.995$ vehicles per hour (accept $52.995$ or $52.996$).

required_elements: Numerical answer approximately $52.995$ vehicles per hour

parts.prompt: Is the rate at which vehicles arrive at the toll plaza at 6 A.M. ($t = 1$) increasing or decreasing? Give a reason for your answer.

scoring_points.description: Concludes that the rate is **increasing** at $t = 1$ because $A'(1) > 0$.

parts.prompt:
The number of vehicles in line at the toll plaza at time $t$, for $a \leq t \leq 4$, is modeled by
$$N(t) = \int_{a}^{t} (A(x) - 400)\,dx$$
where $a$ is the first time in the interval $0 \leq t \leq 5$ at which $A(t) = 400$. To the nearest whole number, find the greatest number of vehicles in line at the toll plaza in the time interval $a \leq t \leq 4$. Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: The equation $A(t) = 400$ has solutions at $t \approx 0.422$ and $t \approx 3.916$ on $[0,5]$. The line first forms at $t = a \approx 0.422$. Since $N'(t) = A(t) - 400$, the number of vehicles is increasing when $A(t) > 400$ and decreasing when $A(t) < 400$. The maximum of $N$ on $[a, 4]$ occurs at $t \approx 3.916$, which is approximately 33 vehicles.

scoring_points.description: Identifies that $N'(t) = A(t) - 400$ and sets $N'(t) = 0$ (i.e., $A(t) = 400$) to find candidates for the maximum, or notes that $N$ is maximized where $A(t)$ changes from greater than 400 to less than 400.

required_elements: Sets $A(t) = 400$ or considers $N'(t) = 0$ to find the candidate maximum

scoring_points.description: Correctly identifies the interior critical point $t \approx 3.916$ within $(a, 4)$ and considers the endpoint $t = 4$ as well (noting $N(a) = 0$).

required_elements: Identifies $t \approx 3.916$ as the interior critical point and checks endpoints

scoring_points.description: Correct answer: $N(3.916) \approx 33$ vehicles (to the nearest whole number). Accept $33$.

required_elements: Answer of $33$ vehicles (or correct numerical answer consistent with student's $a$)

scoring_points.description: Provides correct justification: $N'(t) = A(t) - 400 > 0$ for $t \in (a, 3.916)$ and $N'(t) < 0$ for $t \in (3.916, 4)$, so $N$ has an absolute maximum at $t \approx 3.916$ on $[a, 4]$ (since $N(a) = 0$ and $N(4) < N(3.916)$).

required_elements: Justifies that $N'$ changes from positive to negative at $t \approx 3.916$, confirming the maximum
